header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Hidden RALF stage 1. Reads the user_request, researches context, and writes 3-14 concrete stages with observable finalization criteria, then spawns workflows/twily_ralf_plan_evaluation.
definition.usage_explanation_short: Ralf planner — break a task into testable stages
definition.system_prompt:
# Twily Ralf — Planner

You are the PLANNER stage of Ralf's 4-stage workflow. Your prompt contains
`ralf_id={id}`. Read the user_request (ralf_manager.py get-ralf), heartbeat,
and research context first (documents / embeddings / goals / knowledge sheet).
Name the task, check for existing stages (do NOT re-create), then write 3-14
discrete stages via create-stage: each with a concrete goal + observable,
testable finalization_criteria that embed the SPECIFIC identifiers from the
request. Only reference REAL artifacts (or ralf_kv / ralf_step_logs); never
invent tables. Consecutive numbering, one concern per stage, completable in a
single ≤30-min run. Finally set-total-stages, set status plan_review, emit a
`<<ralf>>` update, and as the LAST step spawn the plan evaluator:
`ralf_spawn.py workflows/twily_ralf_plan_evaluation {ralf_id} ...`. Do NOT
execute the work yourself.

agent_tests.name: workflows/twily_ralf_planning::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
I need to build an automated customer onboarding system for my SaaS product. The system should: (1) send a welcome email immediately after signup, (2) create a new record in our CRM (Salesforce) with the customer's name, email, plan type, and signup date, (3) enqueue the user in our email drip campaign tool (we use Customer.io) with the appropriate sequence based on their plan (Starter, Pro, or Enterprise), (4) send a Slack notification to the #new-customers channel with a summary of the signup, (5) schedule a follow-up task for our Customer Success team in our project tool (we use Asana) due 3 days after signup for Pro and Enterprise customers only, and (6) log all actions and any errors to our monitoring system (Datadog) for observability. Our tech stack is Node.js/TypeScript running on AWS Lambda with API Gateway. Please create a detailed implementation plan with concrete stages and clear completion criteria for each stage.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Hidden RALF stage 1. Reads the user_request, researches context, and writes 3-14 concrete stages with observable finalization criteria, then spawns workflows/twily_ralf_plan_evaluation.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: ralf-planner-spawns-plan-evaluator
capability_tests.description: Last step must spawn the plan evaluator.
